experiments.hypothesis: Post-fix calibration: probability distribution is realistic (not all 99%)
experiments.change_description: Analyze model_prices by run timestamp; split pre-fix vs post-fix
experiments.details: POST-FIX 04:32:31: 0% at 0.99. Dist: 60.7% in 5-10%, 19.8% in 10-20%, 7.1% in 50-70%. 4 contracts pass /bin/zsh.20 floor: SFO-T73 (edge=0.29), MIN-T48 (edge=0.22). Bug confirmed fixed.

experiments.hypothesis: HRRR ensemble: genuine diversity (51 members), not just deterministic duplication
experiments.change_description: Query weather_forecasts member distribution, check ensemble_members_total in model_prices
experiments.details: GENUINE ENSEMBLE: 51 members (member=-1=det, 0-50=ensemble). 398K rows. Members 0-19 full coverage. model_prices avg_total=128, range 30-169.

experiments.hypothesis: Per-city sigma from HRRR: most cities below hardcoded 3.0, 3 cities above
experiments.change_description: Compute std dev of daily max temps across ensemble members per city
experiments.details: Avg sigma=1.87F. Range: Phoenix 0.81F to Atlanta 3.78F. 3 cities above 3.0: ATL(3.78), OKC(3.47), DAL(3.24). Saved to data/city_sigmas.json. Only 2 dates - needs more history.

experiments.hypothesis: Active trades EV: corrected probs reduce EV by ~24%, still positive
experiments.change_description: Compare old model_prob to corrected fair_yes_prob for 36 active paper trades
experiments.details: 36 active paper trades. Corrected EV= (was ). 6 trades negative EV: DEN-B70.5(-), MIA-B81.5(-). 20/36 penny-priced (/bin/zsh.01) - /bin/zsh.20 floor would exclude them. 2 NO-side trades look good post-correction.

experiments.hypothesis: CLI disagrees with hourly on ALL 85 contracts; 16 result flips
experiments.change_description: CLI vs hourly mismatch for 85 contracts

experiments.hypothesis: Penny contracts (<$0.10) are 0%% WR; >=$0.20 is 55.6%%
experiments.change_description: Win rate by price bucket with CLI truth

experiments.hypothesis: Model forecasts consistently HIGH vs CLI (mean error -4.5F)
experiments.change_description: Systematic cold bias in CLI vs model predictions

experiments.hypothesis: Optimal floor $0.40 but only 2 trades; $0.20 is practical optimum
experiments.change_description: Price floor optimization with CLI data

experiments.hypothesis: Model says 0%% but actual is 14.6%%; says 100%% but actual is 16.7%%
experiments.change_description: Model probability calibration check

experiments.hypothesis: Karpathy Round 3 synthesis: Root cause of 7% win rate is systematic trading of near-impossible contracts at $0.01-$0.05. Market is efficient at these prices. Model cold bias (4-15F) compounds the error. Fix: price floor $0.20 + edge ratio cap 0.50 gives 62.5% backtest win rate on 8 trades.
experiments.change_description: Combined filters implemented: MIN_PRICE_FLOOR=0.20, MAX_EDGE_TO_SIZE=0.15, MAX_EDGE_RATIO=0.50, expanded TRADEABLE_CITIES. Waiting for v3/v4 trade settlements to validate.
experiments.details: {"round_3_experiments": 11, "experiments_kept": 3, "code_changes_made": ["MIN_PRICE_FLOOR: 0.10 \u2192 0.20 (EXP-R3-001)", "TRADEABLE_CITIES: expanded to 15 cities (EXP-R3-005)", "MAX_EDGE_TO_SIZE: 0.15 added (EXP-R3-006)", "MAX_EDGE_RATIO: 0.50 added (EXP-R3-009)"], "root_causes_identified": ["54/70 losses were on sub-$0.05 contracts \u2014 market correctly prices near-zero prob", "Model has systematic cold bias 4-15F per city \u2014 actual temps run warmer", "Edge >10% signals model overconfidence, not genuine opportunity", "Edge/price ratio >50% perfectly separates all losers from winners", "Settlement data discrepancy 20.8% \u2014 our actuals differ from Kalshi's ASOS"], "backtest_metrics": {"original_win_rate": "7.14% (5/70)", "filtered_win_rate": "62.5% (5/8) with new filters", "trades_eliminated": 62, "trades_kept": 8}, "caveats": ["Only 8 trades in filtered sample \u2014 overfitting risk is HIGH", "All 70 trades are pipeline_version=1 \u2014 pre-bias correction era", "Must validate against new v3/v4 trades as they settle (Mar 23-26)"], "next_steps": ["Wait for 50+ v3/v4 trades to settle (Mar 23-26)", "Align settlement temperature source with Kalshi ASOS data", "Run Karpathy Round 4 with fresh data after settlements"]}

experiments.hypothesis: Settlement data discrepancy: 20.8% of above/below contracts show different resolution between our hourly-aggregated temperature data and Kalshi official settlement. Our calibration is based on wrong actuals. This corrupts the bias_tracker and forecast_outcomes table.
experiments.change_description: Analysis only. Root cause identified: hourly-aggregation from weather stations differs from Kalshi ASOS settlement data by 1-5F. Need to align to official settlement source.
experiments.details: {"settlement_disagree_count": 11, "settlement_agree_count": 42, "disagree_rate": 0.20754716981132076, "our_yes_kalshi_no": 8, "our_no_kalshi_yes": 3, "interpretation": "In 11/53 (20.8%) above/below contracts, our hourly-aggregated temperature data disagrees with Kalshi's official settlement. Kalshi uses official ASOS/METAR airport data which can differ from our hourly aggregation by 1-5F. This is a significant source of calibration error.", "example_KXLOWTNYC_T48": {"contract": "NYC low temp above 48F?", "our_data": "51.1F \u2192 YES", "kalshi_result": "NO", "delta": "3.1F discrepancy \u2014 Kalshi's ASOS may have measured 47.x"}, "implication": "Our forecast_outcomes calibration is based on wrong actual values. Fix: use Kalshi settlement source or official ASOS direct."}

experiments.hypothesis: Analysis of 50+ active trades against new filter stack. Only 2/36 active trades pass all filters (price>=0.20, edge_ratio<0.5). Both are bucket contracts where warm bias poses risk. Active v4 trades have edge_ratio ~92x - completely outside filters.
experiments.change_description: No code change. Analysis of active portfolio against new parameters. Finding: filters will dramatically reduce new trade entries going forward.
experiments.details: {"active_trades_passing_all_filters": 2, "trade_1": {"ticker": "KXHIGHTBOS-26MAR23-B38.5", "contract": "Will Boston high be 38-39F on Mar 23?", "entry_price": 0.77, "model_prob": 0.953, "edge_ratio": 0.211, "concern": "Boston warm bias +8.5F means actual high likely higher than 38-39F bucket \u2192 possible LOSS", "risk": "medium-high"}, "trade_2": {"ticker": "KXLOWTMIA-26MAR23-B63.5", "contract": "Will Miami low be 63-64F on Mar 23?", "entry_price": 0.83, "model_prob": 0.973, "edge_ratio": 0.16, "concern": "Miami warm bias +5.2F means actual low likely above 64F \u2192 miss bucket \u2192 LOSS", "risk": "medium"}, "insight": "Both passing trades are 'between' (bucket) contracts. Warm bias means actual temps tend to be HIGHER than our model predicts. For bucket contracts, this shifts the probability mass away from predicted bucket."}

experiments.hypothesis: Edge/price ratio < 0.5 perfectly separates winning trades (5/8=62.5%) from losing trades (0/62=0%). This is more principled than absolute edge cap — it normalizes edge by market price, catching overconfident model signals at any price level.
experiments.change_description: Added MAX_EDGE_RATIO=0.50 to strategy.py. Updated position_sizer.py to check both absolute edge cap AND ratio cap. Either cap triggers trade exclusion.
experiments.details: {"finding": "edge/entry_price ratio < 0.5 perfectly separates winners from losers", "ratio_less_0.5": {"n": 8, "wins": 5, "win_rate": 0.625}, "ratio_0.5_to_1.0": {"n": 2, "wins": 0, "win_rate": 0.0}, "ratio_1_to_5": {"n": 7, "wins": 0, "win_rate": 0.0}, "ratio_5_plus": {"n": 53, "wins": 0, "win_rate": 0.0}, "interpretation": "When edge > 50% of market price, the model is predicting something the market has nearly priced out. This is almost always wrong. When edge < 50% of price, the model has a moderate, believable signal.", "implementation": "MAX_EDGE_RATIO = 0.50 added to strategy.py, enforced in position_sizer.py alongside absolute MAX_EDGE_TO_SIZE"}

experiments.hypothesis: MAX_EDGE_TO_SIZE=0.15 may be too restrictive for higher-priced contracts. Active $0.20+ trades have edge 14-42%, and only 1/8 would pass the 15% cap. Need to reconsider edge cap.
experiments.change_description: Analysis of active trades vs new filters. Discovered edge cap conflicts with high-priced contract opportunities. No code change.
experiments.details: {"active_trade_KXHIGHTMIN_T48": {"entry_price": 0.5, "model_prob": 0.79, "raw_edge": 0.29, "bias_corrected_prob": 0.853, "bias_edge": 0.353, "would_pass_edge_cap": false, "note": "Edge 29-35% >> 15% cap"}, "active_trade_KXLOWTMIA_B63.5": {"entry_price": 0.83, "model_prob": 0.97, "raw_edge": 0.14, "note": "Edge 14% < 15% cap \u2192 would pass both filters"}, "insight": "Most active $0.20+ trades have edge 14-42% \u2014 only KXLOWTMIA-B63.5 (edge 13%) passes the MAX_EDGE_TO_SIZE=0.15 cap. This means the edge cap will severely restrict trading in near-term.", "consideration": "May need to raise edge cap for higher-priced contracts, or use relative edge (edge/entry_price ratio) instead of absolute edge.", "alternative_filter": "edge_pct_of_market_price: edge/entry_price < 0.50 rather than absolute edge < 0.15"}

experiments.hypothesis: HIGH_TEMP contracts may outperform LOW_TEMP. In filtered set, high temp shows 4/5 wins (80%). Low temp has 0/30 wins overall, suggesting model calibration is worse for nighttime low temperatures.
experiments.change_description: Analysis only. No code change. Would need larger sample to confirm.
experiments.details: {"low_temp_trades": 18, "low_temp_wins": 0, "low_temp_win_rate": 0.0, "high_temp_trades": 31, "high_temp_wins": 4, "high_temp_win_rate": 0.12903225806451613, "filtered_high_temp": {"n": 5, "wins": 4, "win_rate": 0.8}, "filtered_low_temp": {"n": 1, "wins": 0, "win_rate": 0.0}, "insight": "In filtered set ($0.20+, edge<=15%), high temp contracts show 80% win rate. Low temp sample too small (1 trade). Overall low temp has very low win rate. Model may be better calibrated for high temp than low temp.", "note": "Sample too small for strong conclusions but warrants investigation"}

experiments.hypothesis: Adding max edge cap at 15% combined with $0.20 price floor achieves 62.5% backtest win rate (5/8 trades). Rationale: edge >10% signals model overconfidence on near-impossible contracts. Market pricing at low levels is efficient; our model is just wrong.
experiments.change_description: Added MAX_EDGE_TO_SIZE = 0.15 to strategy.py and enforced in position_sizer.py. Combined with EXP-R3-001 price floor.
experiments.details: {"filter_price_0.20_and_edge_0.15": {"n": 8, "wins": 5, "win_rate": 0.625}, "filter_price_0.20_only": {"n": 9, "wins": 5, "win_rate": 0.556}, "winning_edge_range": {"min": 0.052, "max": 0.093}, "insight": "Adding edge cap at 15% with $0.20 price floor gives 62.5% win rate on 8 trades. The 9th trade (edge=18%) is a loss at entry_price=$0.24. Max edge cap removes the most overconfident trades.", "code_changes": ["strategy.py: MAX_EDGE_TO_SIZE = 0.15", "position_sizer.py: enforce max_edge check"]}

experiments.hypothesis: TRADEABLE_CITIES whitelist is misconfigured. 3/5 wins came from non-whitelisted cities (Denver, SFO, Houston). Hypothesis: removing the city whitelist and relying on the $0.20 price floor will capture more legitimate opportunities.
experiments.change_description: Expand TRADEABLE_CITIES to include all cities OR remove restriction and rely on price floor filter. Active trades at $0.20+ show 6/8 are from non-whitelisted cities.
experiments.details: {"tradeable_cities_whitelist": ["Phoenix", "Dallas", "Seattle", "Chicago"], "wins_from_non_whitelist_cities": ["Denver", "San Francisco", "Houston"], "active_trades_at_020plus": {"Denver": 1, "Boston": 1, "DC": 1, "Minneapolis": 1, "Phoenix": 1, "Seattle": 1, "Austin": 1, "Miami": 1}, "whitelist_passes_at_020plus": ["Phoenix", "Seattle"], "observation": "TRADEABLE_CITIES whitelist is wrong \u2014 3 of 5 wins came from cities NOT in the whitelist (Denver, SFO, Houston). Meanwhile the whitelist allows Phoenix and Seattle which have 0% win rate in backtests.", "recommendation": "Expand or remove TRADEABLE_CITIES restriction. Filter by price floor ($0.20) and edge quality instead.", "proposed_new_whitelist": ["Phoenix", "Dallas", "Seattle", "Chicago", "Denver", "Houston", "San Francisco", "New York", "Los Angeles", "Miami", "Atlanta", "Boston", "Minneapolis", "Washington DC", "Austin"]}

experiments.hypothesis: CRITICAL: Our weather settlement data (hourly-aggregation) differs from Kalshi official settlement. For KXHIGHTHOU (will high be <87F?): our data says 87.8F actual but Kalshi resolved YES. This means our calibration data is corrupted — we are measuring against the wrong temperature source.
experiments.change_description: Identified settlement source discrepancy. No code change yet — need to investigate Kalshi settlement source and align our weather data. Impact: all forecast_outcomes calibration may be based on wrong actuals.
experiments.details: {"houston_anomaly": {"ticker": "KXHIGHTHOU-26MAR21-T87", "contract": "Will max temp be <87F?", "our_actual": 87.8, "kalshi_result": "yes", "our_resolution_source": "hourly-aggregation", "implication": "Kalshi settled YES (temp was <87F) but our data shows 87.8F. Either Kalshi uses official ASOS data that showed lower temp, or our hourly aggregation is wrong."}, "denver_anomaly": {"ticker": "KXHIGHDEN-26MAR21-B86.5", "contract": "Will high be 86-87F?", "our_actual": 84.2, "kalshi_result": "yes", "implication": "Kalshi settled YES (in bucket 86-87) but our data shows 84.2F. Same discrepancy."}, "root_cause": "Our settlement data (hourly-aggregation from weather stations) differs from Kalshi's official settlement source. This means our actual_value in resolutions is WRONG, which invalidates all calibration from forecast_outcomes.", "recommended_fix": "Track Kalshi's official settlement source and use it for calibration, not our hourly aggregation."}

experiments.hypothesis: High edge (>15%) correlates with LOWER win rate. Counter-intuitive finding: edge >10% means 0% win rate. Hypothesis: extreme edge signals model is pricing differently from market on nearly-certain contracts — model overconfidence rather than genuine edge.
experiments.change_description: Analysis only. No code change. Finding: wins cluster at entry_price $0.22-$0.90 with moderate edge 5-10%. All zero-win trades have edge >10% and price <$0.10.
experiments.details: {"edge_5to10_pct": {"n": 19, "wins": 5, "win_rate": 0.263, "avg_price": 0.157}, "edge_10to15_pct": {"n": 5, "wins": 0, "win_rate": 0.0, "avg_price": 0.046}, "edge_15to20_pct": {"n": 10, "wins": 0, "win_rate": 0.0, "avg_price": 0.022}, "edge_20plus_pct": {"n": 36, "wins": 0, "win_rate": 0.0, "avg_price": 0.029}, "insight": "Counter-intuitive: high edge (>10%) correlates with 0% win rate. 5-10% edge shows 26% win rate. High edge is a RED FLAG \u2014 it means market is correctly pricing near-impossible contracts and we're confusing model noise for opportunity.", "conclusion": "Max edge filter: cap edge at 15% to avoid garbage trades. Combined with $0.20 price floor, this should focus on real opportunities.", "strategy_change": "Analyze EDGE_THRESHOLD behavior \u2014 consider capping at 15%"}

experiments.hypothesis: Model cold bias of 5-15F causes systematic mispricing. Hypothesis: warm bias is the ROOT CAUSE of the 7% win rate, not just correlated. Testing: warm bias means we overestimate prob on below-threshold contracts and underestimate on above-threshold.
experiments.change_description: Analysis experiment. Found all 70 settled trades are pipeline_version=1, predating bias correction. The warm bias (actual warmer than model) hurts both above and below contracts depending on threshold placement.
experiments.details: {"forecast_bias_high_contracts": {"mean_error": 4.5, "meaning": "actual temps 4.5F warmer than model"}, "forecast_bias_low_contracts": {"mean_error": 5.1, "meaning": "actual temps 5.1F warmer than model"}, "worst_cities": {"Boston": 8.5, "Minneapolis": 8.0, "DC": 7.8, "Austin": 6.9, "Denver_low": 15.6}, "active_trades_at_0.20_floor": 8, "insight": "All pipeline_version=1 historical trades predate bias correction activation. Bias correction IS coded but may not have been effective when trades were entered. No code change needed \u2014 EXP-R3-001 price floor handles this by filtering out the worst-affected trades at very low prices."}

experiments.hypothesis: Raising min price floor from $0.10 to $0.20 will dramatically improve win rate. Analysis of 70 settled trades shows ZERO wins below $0.20 entry price. All 5 wins occurred at $0.22+. Low-priced contracts are correctly priced by market as near-impossible.
experiments.change_description: Modify strategy.py: MIN_PRICE_FLOOR = 0.10 -> 0.20. Also update position_sizer.py hardcoded value.
experiments.details: {"baseline_trades": 70, "baseline_wins": 5, "baseline_win_rate": 0.07142857142857142, "filtered_trades": 9, "filtered_wins": 5, "filtered_win_rate": 0.5555555555555556, "trades_eliminated": 61, "insight": "54/70 settled trades were at entry_price<$0.05. 0 wins below $0.20. All 5 wins at $0.22+. Raising floor from $0.10 to $0.20 eliminates 61 dead-weight losers.", "backtest_win_rate_at_0.20_floor": 0.5555555555555556, "strategy_change": "MIN_PRICE_FLOOR: 0.10 -> 0.20"}
